page: Microsoft Azure D2als_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Dalsv7 server family. Powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage, relying instead on external storage options, though it is designated as Premium Storage capable. The instance does not feature any integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Operating with a dedicated CPU allocation, the D2als_v7 provides predictable compute resources without the performance variability associated with shared-core or burstable instances. The low-memory configuration of 2.0 GB per core positions this server as a cost-efficient option for compute-aligned workloads that do not require large memory footprints. Because there is no local storage or GPU acceleration, workloads must rely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound processing. This profile is suitable for lightweight web serving, microservices, small-scale CI/CD pipelines, and basic utility applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure D2als_v7 is an entry-level, x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the Dalsv7 family, powered by an AMD processor and running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. This low-memory configuration lacks local storage and GPU resources, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments. With a dedicated CPU allocation, the instance delivers stable, predictable performance for compute-aligned tasks. It represents a cost-efficient option for workloads with modest memory requirements, such as lightweight web servers, microservices, utility applications, and small-scale development or testing environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v7 is a dedicated x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 AMD vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability, designed for low-memory, compute-aligned utility workloads.
